{{WikiProject Qatar|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Yemen|importance=Low}} }} {{Top 25 report|Jun 15 2025 (3rd)|Jun 22 2025 (5th)}} {{Section sizes}} {{User:MiszaBot/config | algo = old(30d) | archive = Talk:Twelve-Day War/Archive %(counter)d | counter = 6 | maxarchivesize = 200K | archiveheader = {{Automatic archive navigator}} | minthreadstoarchive = 1 | minthreadsleft = 4 }} == Merge proposal == Merge [[Background of the Iran–Israel war]] into [[Iran–Israel war]]. The content is completely [[WP:REDUNDANT]] of what's already on [[Iran–Israel war]]. [[WP:PAGELENGTH]] is not a concern because, with the exception of a couple sentences that may or may not have accretive value, there's essentially no net new content that will be added. This specific war is over, so there will likely not be additional valuable coverage of the background that would justify having the need for a [[WP:SPINOFF]] page. [[User:Longhornsg|Longhornsg]] ([[User talk:Longhornsg|talk]]) 20:51, 23 November 2025 (UTC) *'''Support''' All these Wikipedia articles! I wasn't even aware that the background article existed. [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 08:05, 24 November 2025 (UTC) *'''Support''' per proposer, it seems like someone extracted the background content to its own page for some reason. [[User:Smallangryplanet|Smallangryplanet]] ([[User talk:Smallangryplanet|talk]]) 09:52, 24 November 2025 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' per [[WP:SIZERULE]] at 11.5K words. This isn't an ongoing or enormous topic hence this isn't one of those exceptions where this guideline should be ignored. Contrary to the nom, it's not just a case of a few sentences, the current background in this article is 1,052 words, whereas the Background spin off is 2,492 words. We're talking of adding upto 1.5K additional words for an article that {{tq|"probably should be divided or trimmed"}}, not merged. If those 1,500 additional words are redundant then it's better to BLAR via AfD, not merged in order to produce a 13K unwieldy monster article; we have enough of these, we don't need more. For reference I made the split at the time due to the article being too big, trimming away in the process as part of this (hence the size difference). So if anything the background in this article, or other sections not well summarised, should be further trimmed so that the article is a more compact size and adheres better to [[WP:SUMMARYSTYLE]]. [[User:CommunityNotesContributor|CNC]] ([[User talk:CommunityNotesContributor|talk]]) 12:06, 27 November 2025 (UTC) *:{{tq|though the scope of a topic can sometimes justify the added reading material.}} We're talking going to 12k words, with much more culling still apt to do. [[User:Longhornsg|Longhornsg]] ([[User talk:Longhornsg|talk]]) 01:36, 18 December 2025 (UTC) *::Yes based on scope because there are many sub topics to summarise, like [[Gaza war]] which regularly struggles with this conundrum, or [[WWII]] for example. This isn't a [[Template:Major topic editnotice|higher level summary style article]] like those, there is only the Background article along with [[Reactions to the Iran–Israel war]] (that's around 5K words). It should be completely achievable summarising 1 parent and two children to <9K words as the scope is that of a small family. We're not discussing a large family of topics here per the scope, which is what the "sometimes justify" alludes to. Thus I'm proposing trimming (such as Reactions section), not adding here. [[User:CommunityNotesContributor|CNC]] ([[User talk:CommunityNotesContributor|talk]]) 12:56, 18 December 2025 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' per [[WP:SIZERULE]] [[User:JaxsonR|JaxsonR]] ([[User talk:JaxsonR|talk]]) 02:31, 28 November 2025 (UTC) * '''Support''' per nomination. [[User:Achmad Rachmani|Achmad Rachmani]] ([[User talk:Achmad Rachmani|talk]]) 10:43, 12 December 2025 (UTC) *'''Support''' selective merge or possibly even a [[WP:BLAR]]. A merge doesn't mean you have to copy and paste every word of the article wholesale. Regardless, this article probably doesn't need to exist. [[User:Thebiguglyalien|Thebiguglyalien]] ([[User talk:Thebiguglyalien|talk]]) [[Special:Contributions/Thebiguglyalien|🛸]] 22:26, 13 December 2025 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' per [[Wikipedia:Article size]] [[User:Qhairun|Qhairun]] ([[User talk:Qhairun|talk]]) 13:58, 17 December 2025 (UTC) :*'''Strong support''' per nom :[[User:Wikicommonsfan134|Wikicommonsfan134]] ([[User talk:Wikicommonsfan134|talk]]) 10:54, 20 December 2025 (UTC) :'''Support''' per nomination [[User:KashanAbbas|KashanAbbas]] ([[User talk:KashanAbbas|talk]]) 13:15, 31 December 2025 (UTC) *'''Support''' Nothing inherently significant in this spinoff which isn't already covered here or at [[Iran–Israel relations]]. We already have had a slew of articles related to this war AfD'd (many resulting in an rd), I see no inherent difference here. And needn't allude to AfD when the merge processes can also resolve the issue at hand. There have been other problematic POVFORKs like the [[Economic impact of the Iran–Israel war]], which I cleaned up recently (perhaps needs a merger/deletion as well), or other articles significantly POV slanted such as the [[Nuclear program of Iran]] i.e. ARBIPA already struggles with inherent problems as is better limit the scope of articles which further exacerbate them. [[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] ([[User talk:Gotitbro|talk]]) 10:16, 1 January 2026 (UTC) *:@[[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] Strong support and appreciation for your clean up initiative on pages related to this war. [[User:Longhornsg|Longhornsg]] ([[User talk:Longhornsg|talk]]) 03:15, 30 January 2026 (UTC) :'''Support''' per all above [[User:Globetrotter30|Globetrotter30]] ([[User talk:Globetrotter30|talk]]) 16:40, 2 January 2026 (UTC) :'''Support''' given that, as others have said, there doesn't seem to be a point for this article to exist given the main topic article has most information in it already. [[User:Yeoutie|Yeoutie]] ([[User talk:Yeoutie|talk]]) 12:08, 6 January 2026 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' on WP:Article size - on this page we already have 302,000 bytes and 602 citations. It's all the same topic, but jamming everything into one giant page is not going to achieve anything worthwhile. [[User:Moonraker|Moonraker]] ([[User talk:Moonraker|talk]]) 08:19, 4 February 2026 (UTC) :That makes sense. It'll probably be a big overhaul but you know. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-98109-3|~2026-98109-3]] ([[User talk:~2026-98109-3|talk]]) 02:02, 13 February 2026 (UTC) :'''WAIT''' till [[2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran|2026 war]] is over [[User:4-RAZOR 01|4-RΔ𝚉🌑R-01𝕏 ]] ([[User talk:4-RAZOR 01|talk]]) 08:52, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ::now that there is a new war its all the more important we change the name to Twelve Day war to avoid confusion [[User:Hholdenday|Hholdenday]] ([[User talk:Hholdenday|talk]]) 16:20, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ::Why do we need to wait until the 2026 war is over to decide if to merge the background article into this article? [[User:GothicGolem29|GothicGolem29]] [[User talk:GothicGolem29|(Talk)]] 18:16, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Support per nom. [[User:Cfls|Cfls]] ([[User talk:Cfls|talk]]) 18:12, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' as all the content can reasonably by in one place, and when that is possible and there is enough alignment without risk of duplication, then it should be done. [[User:Iljhgtn|Iljhgtn]] ([[Singular they|''they/them'']] · [[User talk:Iljhgtn|talk]]) 18:20, 1 March 2026 (UTC) == EC Edit request: Clear POV pushing in lead == {{Edit extended-protected|Iran–Israel war|answered=yes}} "Israel struck a hospital, and high-rise buildings, including apartments. Iranian missiles struck military and government sites, apartments, a research center, and a hospital. Tightened censorship in Israel made the extent of physical damage unclear." should be something like "Israeli and Iranian attacks struck high rise buildings including apartments, a hospital in each country, research centers, and military sites, but censorship in both countries limited damage assessment." Also, move this to the top of the paragraph where it appears in the lead. This is supported by the exact same citations as the original, and keeps the focus on physical damage. It makes sense to put physical damage listing before you describe the impact of that physical damage, which is why it should be at the top of the paragraph. Reason for the language changes - both struck high rises. Why would you list research and military sites as targets of Iranian strikes when they were also clearly targets of Israeli strikes?? Why would you only cite censorship of Israeli damage when at least in Israel we have good dollar estimates of the physical damage, and where Iran has tightly centered any release of info around damage to nuclear and residential sites, with widely varying death tolls being released from the country indicating a difficulty in accessing information? It is clearly not NPOV for the lead to be written as it is. [[User:Scienceturtle1|Scienceturtle1]] ([[User talk:Scienceturtle1|talk]]) 13:30, 6 February 2026 (UTC) :{{not done}}''':''' Edit requests are for noncontroversial changes only (see [[WP:EDITXY]]). Your proposed change is potentially controversial and will require prior talk page consensus. [[User:Day Creature|Day Creature]] ([[User talk:Day Creature|talk]]) 16:58, 10 February 2026 (UTC) ::Thanks for your reply! I apologize for thinking that parallel descriptions of parallel physical damage is noncontroversial, but I suppose I forgot what website I was on. Thank you for explaining. [[User:Scienceturtle1|Scienceturtle1]] ([[User talk:Scienceturtle1|talk]]) 17:15, 10 February 2026 (UTC) == EC edit request: False and misleading poll interpretation == {{Edit extended-protected|Iran–Israel war|answered=yes}} It is written "Opinion polling on Americans on US involvement in the war by YouGov found 85% of those surveyed were opposed to the war, while 5% supported and 10% were unsure of it." Looking at that source, the question asked (https://today.yougov.com/topics/international/survey-results/daily/2025/06/23/530e0/3) is "do you want the US to be at war with Iran." This makes the sentence in the wikipedia article a lie and severe misinformation, intentional or not. Similar problem in the caption. the question of being at war with iran is DIFFERENT from morally/emotionally supporting Israel in the war and is different from economically or militarily supporting Israel in the war, which encapsulates all the different possible incorrect interpretations of the currently writing. The sentence must be changed to align with the poll. Proper sentence aligning with the poll question is ""Opinion polling on Americans on US involvement in the war by YouGov found 85% of those surveyed were opposed to the US going to war with Iran during the Israel-Iran War, while 5% supported and 10% were unsure of it." The graphic must also be changed. The graphic states "Poll of Americans on if the US should become involved in conflict with Iran." It appears to have been created by Des Vallee as their own work. However, self-reproductions of a poll which do not match the poll wording are inherently misinformation, intentionally or unintentionally, given the huge impact of framing in poll questions and the perception that pie chart images from polls which show a question would be expected to reflect the actual question posed to participants. The caption and graphic should both say "Opinion polling on the US going to war with Iran during the Israel-Iran War." This is the actual poll. This is all the more obvious because AFTER the US strikes, only 40% of the US according to the same poll thought the US was at war with Iran. https://today.yougov.com/topics/international/survey-results/daily/2025/06/23/530e0/2. Might be worth mentioning although outside the scope of this edit request. I should say it is disappointing that this clear misinformation has been up for so long. Given the extent, I believe it would be fair to add Wikipedia to the "Misinformation" section of the article, although I'll leave that determination to more experienced editors as I understand we don't like to cite the sources publishing evidence to this effect.[[User:Scienceturtle1|Scienceturtle1]] ([[User talk:Scienceturtle1|talk]]) 13:47, 6 February 2026 (UTC) :{{partly done}}''':''' I've changed the wording of the sentence in question to be a bit more precise. (The image can't be edited directly so you'll have to raise your concerns at the talk page for it on Commons). However, this was at most a minor lack of clarity and precision in wording, and calling it "a lie and severe misinformation" is frankly absurd. As is suggesting that Wikipedia should be added to the Misinformation section of the article. If you are going to make edit requests in the Arab–Israeli conflict topic area, I would suggest rethinking how you approach things. [[User:Day Creature|Day Creature]] ([[User talk:Day Creature|talk]]) 17:13, 10 February 2026 (UTC) ::Hi Day Creature, I will simply say that I work in science and if someone published a paper which re-worded a poll question this severely they'd face career-threatening investigations and retraction. Thank you for partially updating though. [[User:Scienceturtle1|Scienceturtle1]] ([[User talk:Scienceturtle1|talk]]) 17:18, 10 February 2026 (UTC) == Iranian tactical/strategic victory == this war should be Iran's tactical/strategic victory Reasons: 1: The US and Israel were failed to destroy Iran's nuclear program and facilities in this war, and now the US is negotiating with Iran again to have Iran stop enriching uranium and hand over the uranium. 2: Israel and the US failed to change the current Iranian regime in the war And if the US and Israel have destroyed Iran's nuclear facilities, there is no reason for them to want to negotiate with Iran again about its nuclear program, right? [[User:Mamadalidragon|Mamadalidragon]] ([[User talk:Mamadalidragon|talk]]) 07:37, 18 February 2026 (UTC) :Sources need to say that. [[User:JaxsonR|JaxsonR]] ([[User talk:JaxsonR|talk]]) 20:34, 27 February 2026 (UTC) == Requested move 19 February 2026 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] {{strong|after}} discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''moved.''' Consensus favors move, "Twelve-Day War" preferred by editors over 12-Day War —[[User:Ganesha811|Ganesha811]] ([[User talk:Ganesha811|talk]]) 12:33, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ---- [[:Iran–Israel war]] → {{no redirect|Twelve-Day War}} – Twelve-Day War is now the universally accepted [[WP:COMMONNAME]] for this war. * Sources: [https://www.france24.com/en/middle-east/20250805-twelve-day-war-impact-of-iranian-strikes-censored-israel France 24]; [https://apnews.com/article/iran-israel-us-trump-military-carrier-war-931c25411eeef7d8cee679b3544b792a AP]; [https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/02/01/iran-missiles-persian-gulf-trump-military-buildup/ Washington Post]; [https://www.cfr.org/event/us-policy-options-after-twelve-day-war CFR]; [https://www.aa.com.tr/en/world/iaea-chief-says-iran-inspections-deal-possible-but-terribly-difficult-after-12-day-war/3829539 IAEA]; [https://newlinesinstitute.org/middle-east-center/punishing-vulnerability-irans-minority-crackdown-after-the-12-day-war/ NewLines]; [https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/dispatches/iran-poses-challenges-to-a-national-security-strategy-ready-to-move-on/ Atlantic Council]; [https://mei.edu/publication/irans-axis-of-resistance-after-the-12-day-war-adaptation-restructuring-and-reconstitution/ Middle East Institute]; [https://carnegieendowment.org/russia-eurasia/politika/2025/11/russia-iran-deterrence-plans Carnegie]; [https://europeanleadershipnetwork.org/commentary/the-non-proliferation-outlook-after-the-twelve-day-war-moving-beyond-damage-control/ European Leadership Network]; [https://www.fpri.org/article/2025/10/humiliation-and-transformation-the-islamic-republic-after-the-12-day-war/ FPRI]; [https://www.lrb.co.uk/blog/2026/february/no-going-back London Review of Books]; [https://warontherocks.com/2025/11/saudi-nuclear-posture-after-the-12-day-war/ War on the Rocks] [[User:NotBartEhrman|NotBartEhrman]] ([[User talk:NotBartEhrman|talk]]) 13:55, 19 February 2026 (UTC) — '''''Relisting.''''' ''[[User:TarnishedPath|TarnishedPath]]''[[User talk:TarnishedPath|talk]] 08:44, 27 February 2026 (UTC) * '''Support''': furthermore it serves as a natural disambiguation to the broader [[Iran–Israel proxy conflict]] and other articles known as the [[Iran–Israel conflict]]. ―[[User:Howardcorn33|Howard]] • [[User talk:Howardcorn33|🌽33]] 15:25, 19 February 2026 (UTC) * '''Support''' - "Twelve-Day War" has become the common name in the months that have followed the war. [[User:Guz13|Guz13]] ([[User talk:Guz13|talk]]) 22:05, 20 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' unless there is a more notable "Twelve-Day War" that I am unaware of, but from [[WP:COMMONNAME]], this should be the title based on the majority of [[WP:BESTSOURCES]]. I think, as I say, unless there is some war that uses this even more commonly, but even then, that article should instead already then be titled with "Twelve-Day War", so that seems unlikely to me. [[User:Iljhgtn|Iljhgtn]] ([[Singular they|''they/them'']] · [[User talk:Iljhgtn|talk]]) 05:25, 21 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' – Per [[WP:COMMONNAME]] and [[WP:AT]], article titles should reflect the name most frequently used in reliable, independent secondary sources. Major international outlets such as France 24, Associated Press, and The Washington Post, along with policy and research institutions including Council on Foreign Relations and International Atomic Energy Agency, now predominantly use “Twelve-Day War” when referring to this conflict. The proposed title is concise, recognizable, and as stated above, provides clear natural disambiguation from the broader Iran–Israel conflict. [[User:ScottyNolan|ScottyNolan]] ([[User talk:ScottyNolan|talk]]) 20:50, 21 February 2026 (UTC) [[User:ScottyNolan|ScottyNolan]] ([[User talk:ScottyNolan|talk]]) 20:50, 21 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''': broader disambiguation '''[[:User:CROIX|CROIX]]'''[[:User talk:CROIX|talk]] 23:40, 21 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' per commonly used name along with the fact that title is precise and accurate. Also stays separate from Iran Israel War. [[User:8bit12man|8bit12man]] ([[User talk:8bit12man|talk]]) 02:32, 23 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' – as the US was also a major belligerent in this war, [[Twelve-Day War]], which is more common than the current title, is also more clear. [[Special:Contributions/Chicdat|🐔]] [[User:Chicdat|Chicdat]]  ''[[User talk:Chicdat|Bawk to me!]]'' 14:04, 23 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' "Twelve Day War" is clearly not a [[WP:COMMONNAME]]. In news sources, 12-day (and not "Twelve Day") is used descriptively or as shorthand and not as the the proper name for the war. For example, in the AP article the conflict is also referred to as {{tq|12-day Israel-Iran war}} and {{tq|June 13-24 war}}, which by itself rebuts the argument of universal acceptance. Similarly the France24, Washington Post, and AA articles use all lower case for {{tq|12-day war}}. [https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/11/irans-leaders-rail-against-us-sedition-in-1979-revolution-celebrations Al Jazeera] does the same. American foreign policy thinktanks seem to be the only ones using "Twelve Day War". However, [https://www.brookings.edu/articles/the-road-to-the-israel-iran-war/ Brookings] previously used Israel-Iran, [https://acleddata.com/qa/qa-twelve-days-shook-region-inside-iran-israel-war ACLED] used Iran-Israel war, and [https://www.hudson.org/defense-strategy/how-israels-operation-rising-lion-dismantled-iran-within-case-study-art-deception Hudson] used Operation Rising Lion. [https://www.washingtoninstitute.org/sites/default/files/pdf/PolicyFocus182Eisenstadtv3.pdf WINEP] uses Israel-Iran war in graphics and notes that it {{tq|later came to be informally known as the twelve-day war}}. [[User:EvansHallBear|EvansHallBear]] ([[User talk:EvansHallBear|talk]]) 22:18, 23 February 2026 (UTC) *:I don't think it's accurate to say that the upper case version is ''only'' used by American foreign policy thinktanks. Besides the LRB article I linked, here's a [https://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2025/08/04/israels-zones-of-denial ''New Yorker'' article] that introduces the upper case term and then uses it consistently. I also find the upper case version is used in Israeli and Iranian sources; e.g. [https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/413871 Israeli Channel 7], [https://www.tehrantimes.com/news/518186/Iran-approves-museum-dedicated-to-12-day-war-with-Israel Tehran Times], [https://nournews.ir/en/news/247034/Leader-Unity-of-Iranian-Nation-in-Twelve-Day-War-frustrated-the-enemy Nour News quoting Khamenei]. [[User:NotBartEhrman|NotBartEhrman]] ([[User talk:NotBartEhrman|talk]]) 22:46, 23 February 2026 (UTC) *::+1 @[[User:NotBartEhrman|NotBartEhrman]] [[User:Iljhgtn|Iljhgtn]] ([[Singular they|''they/them'']] · [[User talk:Iljhgtn|talk]]) 00:31, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *::Fair enough. My point stands that you are conflating "12-day" as a descriptor and "Twelve Day War" as a title to make the latter appear more common than it really is. [[User:EvansHallBear|EvansHallBear]] ([[User talk:EvansHallBear|talk]]) 01:32, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *:::This is more akin to [[Six-Day War]], which ''could'' conceivably also be called 1967 Arab–Israeli war, but then no one would know what you are talking about and that would not be anywhere near the COMMONNAME, similarly here [[Twelve-Day War]] is the much more COMMONNAME standard colloquial label for this 12-day period of conflict. [[User:Iljhgtn|Iljhgtn]] ([[Singular they|''they/them'']] · [[User talk:Iljhgtn|talk]]) 01:36, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *::::These are not comparable, as there's been numerous Arab–Israeli wars, while this is the only Iran–Israel war. Also, while 12-day war is a common description of the war, there's no evidence that there's a common name. Lots of sources say for example "Iran's 12-day war with Israel", "the 12-day Iran–Israel war" or "12-day war between Iran and Israel"; I haven't seen many sources using it as a proper name. [[User:9ninety|9ninety]] ([[User talk:9ninety|talk]]) 04:51, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' Per nom, Twelve-Day War is the common name. [[User:JaxsonR|JaxsonR]] ([[User talk:JaxsonR|talk]]) 03:59, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' per EvansHallBear. Most sources use 12-day as a descriptor and not as a name. The capitalized form "Twelve-Day War" is very rare. No evidence of a common name. A title consisting of the primary combatants is far more useful and informative than the length of the conflict. There's also no need for disambiguation, as this is the only Iran–Israel war. [[User:9ninety|9ninety]] ([[User talk:9ninety|talk]]) 04:56, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *:I think "very rare" is unfair at this point: I've found examples in Israeli, Iranian and third-party think tank sources, which are the three kinds of sources I can think of. [[User:NotBartEhrman|NotBartEhrman]] ([[User talk:NotBartEhrman|talk]]) 13:49, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *::You listed three Israeli and Iranian sources in your previous comment; in the second source, 12-day war is used multiple times as a descriptor, and "Twelve-Day War" is only mentioned once as an alternative name. The third source uses it when quoting Khamenei, but uses the descriptive 12-day war in prose. And the first source still says "Israel's Twelve-Day War against Iran", instead of just "the Twelve-Day War". The point is, not only is it not the common name, it's also ambiguous, as sources always have to qualify it with the combatants to disambiguate. [[User:9ninety|9ninety]] ([[User talk:9ninety|talk]]) 15:27, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' I've been thinking about this for a while and the weight of the sources seems clearly to be using '''Twelve-Day War''' as the descriptive title, per [[WP:COMMONNAME]]. In addition to the dozen strong reliable and verifiable sources cited above by [[User:NotBartEhrman]], and the several others listed by other editors here, the [https://www.fpri.org/article/2025/10/humiliation-and-transformation-the-islamic-republic-after-the-12-day-war/ Foreign Policy Research Institute], [https://www.osw.waw.pl/en/publikacje/osw-commentary/2025-12-18/iran-crisis-landscape-after-twelve-day-war Centre for Eastern Studies], [https://www.cissm.umd.edu/research-impact/publications/iranian-public-opinion-soon-after-twelve-day-war Center for International & Security Studies at Maryland], [https://www.defensa.gob.es/documents/2073105/2726226/la_guerra_de_los_doce_dias_2025_dieeea51_eng.pdf/cf02b9ed-7989-ed8c-5cb6-c7c943d75d0e?t=1751962866372 Instituto Español de Estudios Estratégicos (on the Spanish Defense Ministry website)]. The sources are from around the globe and across the political spectrum. The weight of the evidence seems clear that we should follow the dictate of WP:COMMONNAME to "Use commonly recognizable names"; in this case, this is the recognizable name for the conflict. [[User:Alansohn|Alansohn]] ([[User talk:Alansohn|talk]]) 21:14, 24 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' per [[WP:COMMONNAME]]. [[User:Skitash|Skitash]] ([[User talk:Skitash|talk]]) 21:23, 24 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Relisting comment''': EvansHallBear and 9ninety have both argued that "12-day" is more common than "Twelve-Day" A review of the sources provided by NotBartEhrman shows mixed usage and so there might be some validity to the argument. There is support for a move; however editors need to determine where best to move to. ''[[User:TarnishedPath|TarnishedPath]]''[[User talk:TarnishedPath|talk]] 08:44, 27 February 2026 (UTC) ::I think Twelve-Day War is better as the title than 12-day War, but 12-day War can serve as a redirect to Twelve-Day War when this closes out. [[User:Iljhgtn|Iljhgtn]] ([[Singular they|''they/them'']] · [[User talk:Iljhgtn|talk]]) 14:23, 27 February 2026 (UTC) ::My concern was less over "12-day" versus "Twelve-Day", but that both terms are being used descriptively and not as the name of the war. E.g. the AP article cited by NotBartEhrman calls it the {{tq|12-day Israel-Iran war}}. Claims that this usage supports "Twelve-Day War" as a common name are illogical. That the two terms are used interchangeably also supports the argument that a [[WP:COMMONNAME]] has yet to develop. ::As another example, just yesterday +972mag published a [https://www.972mag.com/israel-iran-escalation-regional-war/ piece] referring to the war as {{tq|Israel’s war with Iran in June}} and not either of the proposed "common names". [[User:EvansHallBear|EvansHallBear]] ([[User talk:EvansHallBear|talk]]) 20:29, 27 February 2026 (UTC) :Note: [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Western Asia|WikiProject Western Asia]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Jordan|WikiProject Jordan]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Death|WikiProject Death]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Qatar|WikiProject Qatar]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject United States|WikiProject United States]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Israel|WikiProject Israel]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Military history/Post-Cold War task force|WikiProject Military history/Post-Cold War task force]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Iran|WikiProject Iran]], [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Military history|WikiProject Military history]], and [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Yemen|WikiProject Yemen]] have been notified of this discussion. ''[[User:TarnishedPath|TarnishedPath]]''[[User talk:TarnishedPath|talk]] 08:45, 27 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' per nom, no preference between "12-day" or "Twelve-Day".--[[User:Ortizesp|Ortizesp]] ([[User talk:Ortizesp|talk]]) 15:25, 27 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Support''' [[User:Historyguy1138|Historyguy1138]] ([[User talk:Historyguy1138|talk]]) 20:38, 27 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Support''' [[Talk:Iran–Israel war#c-ScottyNolan-20260221205000-NotBartEhrman-20260219135500|per ScottyNolan]] & sources in nom. [[User:Justanotherguy54|Justanotherguy54]] ([[User talk:Justanotherguy54|talk]]) 21:11, 27 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' unambiguous title. --[[User:Syd Storm|Syd Storm]] ([[User talk:Syd Storm|talk]]) 11:35, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Support''' per nomination. Regarding the talk about capitalised form, or the way it should be named, I think the one suggested is the best. Afterall, we do have the ''1967 Arab–Israeli war'' article named as [[:Six-Day War]]. '''[[User:Shaan Sengupta|Shaan Sengupta]][[User talk:Shaan Sengupta|Talk]]''' 12:12, 28 February 2026 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
== Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 26 February 2026 == {{edit extended-protected|Iran–Israel war|answered=yes}} I want to include the Iranian casualty estimate for Israel, because I believe that these numbers simply do not add up, and would not be fair and accurate, since the reports claim 1 died the first day, 5 the next, 9 the next, 24 the next, 1 after, and then 2 in hospital, which doesn't add up to 28 to 33, and there was a military censorship, so I believe it's only fair and would make the article more accurate. [[User:Youngmonkeyblud|Youngmonkeyblud]] ([[User talk:Youngmonkeyblud|talk]]) 04:58, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :[[File:Red question icon with gradient background.svg|20px|link=|alt=]] '''Not done:''' it's not clear what changes you want made. Please detail the specific changes in a [[Wikipedia:Edit requests#General considerations|"change X to Y" format]] and provide a [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources|reliable source]] if appropriate. [[User:Day Creature|Day Creature]] ([[User talk:Day Creature|talk]]) 05:34, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Continuance or new article == So these strikes just happened. Would they be included in this article, as a continuance i.e ceasefire over, same war? OR new article, potentially new term and even "conflict". Thanks. :) [[Special:Contributions/~2026-10111-9|~2026-10111-9]] ([[User talk:~2026-10111-9|talk]]) 07:07, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :diffrent war, different articles [[User:4-RAZOR 01|4-RΔ𝚉🌑R-01𝕏 ]] ([[User talk:4-RAZOR 01|talk]]) 08:48, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Has its own article, [[2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran]]. [[User:Aydoh8|Aydo]][[User talk:Aydoh8|h8]][[Special:Contributions/Aydoh8|[what have I done now?]]] 09:00, 28 February 2026 (UTC) == Why is Iran-Israel war label 'ran-Israel'? == The title is pretty self-explainationary [[User:Alimsts|Alimsts]] ([[User talk:Alimsts|talk]]) 12:38, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :*self-explanatory :[[User:Alimsts|Alimsts]] ([[User talk:Alimsts|talk]]) 12:39, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :(@[[User:Ganesha811|Ganesha811]] fixed it) [[User:Alimsts|Alimsts]] ([[User talk:Alimsts|talk]]) 12:40, 28 February 2026 (UTC) == Requested move 28 February 2026 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] {{strong|after}} discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''not moved.''' ([[Wikipedia:Requested moves/Closing instructions#Closure by a page mover|closed by non-admin page mover]]) —[[User:Compassionate727|Compassionate727]] ([[User talk:Compassionate727|T]]·[[Special:Contributions/Compassionate727|C]]) 22:12, 28 February 2026 (UTC) ---- [[:Twelve-Day War]] → {{no redirect|2025 Twelve-Day War}} – To more clearly identify the year of the incident and reduce confusion. [[User:Cfls|Cfls]] ([[User talk:Cfls|talk]]) 18:11, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose'''. [[WP:PRECISION]] says:"Usually, titles should unambiguously define the topical scope of the article, but should be no more precise than that." There is only one twelve day war so including the year in the title is not necessary as the tiouc can be defined without it. [[User:GothicGolem29|GothicGolem29]] [[User talk:GothicGolem29|(Talk)]] 18:27, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *:'''Oppose''' no reason to add extra specific date [[User:8bit12man|8bit12man]] ([[User talk:8bit12man|talk]]) 19:05, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Note: [[Wikipedia talk:WikiProject Military history|WikiProject Military history]] has been notified of this discussion. [[User:Cfls|Cfls]] ([[User talk:Cfls|talk]]) 18:33, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' - this is unnecessary, what other Twelve-Day Wars are being disambiguated against? Does [[American Civil War]] need to be moved as well? - [[User:Indefensible|Indefensible]] ([[User talk:Indefensible|talk]]) 19:13, 28 February 2026 (UTC) *'''Strong oppose and possibly snow close'''. It seems like there is a general massive reluctance among the Wikipedia community to assign proper, non-placeholder names to recent conflicts. This article has finally been given a proper, non-descriptive, and unambiguous name and there is no good reason to try to subvert this. It doesn't seem like this suggestion will gain much support and it would be preferrable to not have to keep this template up for seven days (especially as this page is likely to experience an increase in views in the coming days) unless any serious support arises. '''''[[:User:An anonymous username, not my real name|— An anonymous username,]] [[:User talk:An anonymous username, not my real name|not my real name]]''''' 19:47, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' There is no other war also called the Twelve-Day War, so adding 2025 to the name is unnecessary. [[User:Christianhatley527|Christianhatley527]] ([[User talk:Christianhatley527|talk]]) 19:51, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Oppose and probable snow close''': there is nothing to disambiguate from. ―[[User:Howardcorn33|Howard]] • [[User talk:Howardcorn33|🌽33]] 20:11, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Comment''' I agree only if the [[2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran|current conflict]] also lasts 12 days :) [[User:Dasomm|Dasomm]] ([[User talk:Dasomm|talk]]) 20:19, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Snow close''' - Twelve-Day War is the common name, there's no need to disambiguate. [[User:BappleBusiness|BappleBusiness]][[User talk:BappleBusiness|[talk]]] 20:22, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' There’s no need for disambiguation. [[User:Blaylockjam10|Blaylockjam10]] ([[User talk:Blaylockjam10|talk]]) 21:55, 28 February 2026 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
== "[[:Iran-Israel-United States war]]" listed at [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|Redirects for discussion]] == [[File:Information.svg|30px]] The redirect [//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Iran-Israel-United_States_war&redirect=no Iran-Israel-United States war] has been listed at [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion|redirects for discussion]] to determine whether its use and function meets the [[Wikipedia:Redirect|redirect guidelines]]. Readers of this page are welcome to comment on this redirect at '''{{section link|1=Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion/Log/2026 March 1#Iran-Israel-United States war}}''' until a consensus is reached. [[User:Abesca|Abesca]] ([[User talk:Abesca|talk]]) 21:12, 1 March 2026 (UTC) == "Suicide drones" == I think this is loaded language, as the drones do not commit suicide. Just like any other munition doesn't. Would we say "suicide cruise missile"? I suggest calling them explosive drones. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13410-99|~2026-13410-99]] ([[User talk:~2026-13410-99|talk]]) 09:24, 2 March 2026 (UTC) == Untitled comment == WHY ON EARTH is this called "Twelve-Day War"? I get that it's a ripoff of the 1967 war, but why? Why not call it the 2025 Iran war (nice and simple), or the Iran-Israel war? What about other wars that last a similar period? Will we start referring to every war now by the number of days? Literally NOBODY calls it the "Twelve-Day War", whoever came up with that and unilaterally plastered it on Wikipedia should be banned, absolutely ridiculous stuff. I get that it's an "encyclopedia anybody can edit", but have some semblance of standards. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/~2026-66032-1|~2026-66032-1]] ([[User talk:~2026-66032-1#top|talk]]) 17:37, 2 March 2026 (UTC) :We go by what most reliable sources call it. If you don't like the name, take it up with the sources. - [[User:ZLEA|ZLEA]] [[User talk:ZLEA|T]]ǀ[[Special:Contributions/ZLEA|C]] 01:38, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :Both Israel and Iran call it the "Twelve-Day War," as do American think tanks and, incidentally, the US President. [[User:NotBartEhrman|NotBartEhrman]] ([[User talk:NotBartEhrman|talk]]) 01:42, 3 March 2026 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 3 March 2026 == {{edit extended-protected|Twelve-Day War|answered=yes}} Ali Khamenei is now dead and was killed. a X needs to be added next to his name in the War leaders list so it shows that he was assassinated. [[User:Adonyistdebest|Adonyistdebest]] ([[User talk:Adonyistdebest|talk]]) 04:18, 3 March 2026 (UTC) :{{done}} [[User:Toast1454|'''Toast1454''']][[User talk:Toast1454|'''T''']][[Special:Contributions/Toast1454|'''C''']] 12:44, 3 March 2026 (UTC) == USS The Sullivans == The mention of USS The Sullivans links to the Allen M. Sumner destroyer, not the Arleigh Burke DDG. [[User:Decooldude|Decooldude]] ([[User talk:Decooldude|talk]]) 02:17, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :Sorry, it links to the Fletcher-class, not the AMS. [[User:Decooldude|Decooldude]] ([[User talk:Decooldude|talk]]) 02:18, 4 March 2026 (UTC) == No explanation of how the war got its name == Back when this article was titled "Iran–Israel war" there was a section that mentioned how Trump had coined the name "Twelve-Day War". Now there is no mention of this in the article. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123 ]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 02:55, 4 March 2026 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 4 March 2026 == {{edit extended-protected|Twelve-Day War|answered=y}} Add the assassination symbol next to Ali Khamenei [[User:Fighiant|Fighiant]] ([[User talk:Fighiant|talk]]) 08:32, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :What symbol are you talking about?[[User:Slomo666|Slomo666]] ([[User talk:Slomo666|talk]]) 12:44, 4 March 2026 (UTC) ::The "X" symbol [[User:Fighiant|Fighiant]] ([[User talk:Fighiant|talk]]) 12:57, 4 March 2026 (UTC) :::It was already correctly removed by @[[User:Foxhound03|Foxhound03]] since it didnt happen in 2025. [[User:NotBartEhrman|NotBartEhrman]] ([[User talk:NotBartEhrman|talk]]) 13:06, 4 March 2026 (UTC) ::::In that case,

[[File:X mark.svg|20px|link=|alt=]] '''Not done for now:''' please establish a [[Wikipedia:Consensus|consensus]] for this alteration '''[[Wikipedia:Edit requests#Planning a request|before]]''' posting an edit request.

Happy editing, [[User:Slomo666|Slomo666]] ([[User talk:Slomo666|talk]]) 13:46, 4 March 2026 (UTC)
